Fn::Base64 accepts plaintext .. and outputs Base64 encoded text UserData: Fn::Base64: !Sub \/\ yum -y update i yum -y install httpd amazon-linux-extras install -y php7.2 amazon-linux-extras install epel -y HL IR R L B T T BRI ToE T e o To T SRS § A N T round—cxe:#sbgcolor;\“>" A WA AR IVAT LS 4 1§ : echo "
Instance ID :s{Instance.InstanceId}/h1>

" >> /var/www/html/index.html | ;echo "
" >> /var/www/html/index.html echo "" >> /var/www/html/index.html Fn::Sub substitutes in the input, with their actual 5 In this example the of the Instance logical resource ${Parameter} ${ }O# }
